canonical_question: How does consistent hashing work and when to use it?
aliases: how to distribute keys across nodes evenly
when to use consistent hashing vs rendezvous hashing
constraints: Hash function must be uniform and deterministic (e.g., MD5, SHA-1, xxHash) — non-uniform hashing destroys load balance
Virtual nodes are mandatory for production — bare ring hashing gives O(log n) expected max-to-average load ratio
Do not use consistent hashing for clusters with fewer than 3 nodes — simple modular hashing is sufficient
Node removal requires reassigning only the departing node's keys to its successor — never rehash the entire ring
Hash ring metadata must be replicated to all clients/proxies — stale ring state causes misrouted requests

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to partition data by key range (alphabetical, time-based)
Static set of buckets that never changes
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Jump hash (Google, 2014) — perfect balance, zero memory, O(ln n)

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need weighted routing with replication factor k
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Rendezvous (HRW) hashing — simpler, O(n) per lookup, no ring metadata

inputs_needed.question: What type of system are you distributing across?
How many nodes (servers) are in your cluster?
Do you need data replicated across multiple nodes?
